questions.audio_script: Why did the six residents have to find another place to stay?
options: A) Due to a fire alarm in their apartments.
B) Because of the smoke and heat damage.
C) Due to the water used to extinguish the flames.
D) Because of the collapse of the three-story building.
questions.key_sentence: Due to smoke and heat damage, the four apartments were declared uninhabitable, and the six residents had to move to another place, officials said. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 事实细节题。新闻提到，由于烟雾和高温造成的损坏，公寓被宣布为不宜居住，导致居民必须搬离。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: What does the news report say the Norwalk Fire Marshal is doing?
options: A) Investigating the cause of the incident.
B) Helping search for the suspect of the crime.
C) Rescuing the businessmen trapped in the building.
D) Checking town records for the property developer.
questions.key_sentence: The Norwalk Fire Marshal is investigating the cause and origin of the fire. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 事实细节题。新闻最后一句明确指出，消防局局长正在调查火灾的原因和源头。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: What have a number of past studies found about season of birth?
options: A) It plays a less important role in one's health than nutrient intake.
B) It impacts people's health to a lesser degree than sun exposure.
C) It is associated with people's mental health conditions.
D) It is linked with older adults' symptoms of depression.
questions.key_sentence: A number of past studies have found the link between season of birth and mental health diagnoses. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 事实细节题。文中提到，过去的一些研究发现了出生季节与精神健康诊断之间的联系。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: What did the new study find about the relationship between participants' month of birth and symptoms of depression?
options: A) It was indefinite.
B) It was systematic.
C) It was straightforward.
D) It was insignificant.
questions.key_sentence: Overall, the new study found no significant relationship between participants' month of birth and symptoms of depression or anxiety. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 事实细节题。新研究发现，出生月份与抑郁或焦虑症状之间没有显著关系（no significant），对应 D 项（微不足道的）。 [cite: 1]

questions.subcategory: 生物技术与职业犬培育
questions.audio_script: What do the researchers' tests show about the cloned dog?
options: A) It has helped solve several murder cases.
B) It has become a star police dog in Beijing.
C) It has surpassed its mother in performance.
D) It has done better than naturally born dogs.
questions.key_sentence: The clone has already performed better than traditionally bred dogs on several tests. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 事实细节题。测试显示，克隆犬的表现已经优于传统饲养（即自然出生）的警犬。 [cite: 1]

questions.subcategory: 生物技术与职业犬培育
questions.audio_script: What is the scientists' purpose in cloning police dogs?
options: A) To speed up investigation into criminal cases.
B) To test the feasibility of cloning technology.
C) To cut down training expenses.
D) To reduce their training time.
questions.key_sentence: If the clone continues to perform as well as expected, it could mean a huge reduction in the training time for police dogs, which usually takes about five years. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 目的理解题。科学家克隆警犬是为了缩短原本长达五年的训练时间。 [cite: 1]

questions.subcategory: 生物技术与职业犬培育
questions.audio_script: Why does the news report say the scientists' goal is not yet possible?
options: A) Cloning is too complicated a process.
B) The technology is yet to be accepted.
C) Cloning is ethically controversial.
D) The technology is too expensive.
questions.key_sentence: However, this goal is not yet possible due to the current costs of the technology. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 原因分析题。新闻提到，由于目前技术成本（costs）过高，缩短训练时间的目标尚无法实现。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: How did the man get to know about the book The Three Body Problem?
options: A) He read it somewhere online.
B) He heard about it from a coworker.
C) He read an article reviewing it.
D) He watched a TV series based on it.
questions.key_sentence: A colleague mentioned the book, but I've been so busy writing my thesis that I haven't been able to read for pleasure in months. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 细节获取题。男士提到是一位同事（colleague）向他提到了这本书。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: What does the woman say she can't remember about the book's author?
options: A) His publications.
B) His first book.
C) His address.
D) His name.
questions.key_sentence: I can't remember his name, but he's written three books in all, and The Three Body Problem is the first in the series. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 细节记忆题。女士明确表示她不记得作者的名字（name）了。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: What does the man have to do to finish his thesis?
options: A) Collect a lot more data.
B) Relax a bit less often.
C) Clarify many new concepts.
D) Read more reference books.
questions.key_sentence: I'm a bit overwhelmed with the amount of data I still need to collect to finish my thesis. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 任务理解题。男士提到他还需要收集大量数据（data）来完成论文。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: What will the man most probably do first after the conversation?
options: A) Find out the show's most interesting episodes.
B) Watch the series together with the woman.
C) Get an e-copy of the book to read.
D) Check to see when the show starts.
questions.key_sentence: Well, maybe you could download an electronic copy of the book and try to read it before the show starts. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 行动预测题。女士建议他下载电子书阅读，男士认为这是个好主意。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: Why does the woman call the man?
options: A) To check the prices of his farm produce.
B) To ask the way to the Newcastle City Hall.
C) To inquire about the vegetarian food festival.
D) To seek the man's help with her work on the farm.
questions.key_sentence: I have an inquiry to make. It's about the vegetarian food festival you are holding on the 19th of August at the Newcastle City Hall. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 目的识别题。女士致电是咨询关于即将举办的素食节（vegetarian food festival）的情况。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: What is the man still looking for?
options: A) Bakers.
B) Vendors.
C) Vegetarians.
D) Organisers.
questions.key_sentence: It says on your website that you are still looking for vendors, and I grow organic vegetables on my farm, as well as doing my own home baking. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 需求定位题。女士提到网站显示还在招募供应商（vendors）。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: What does the man say they are getting close to?
options: A) The issuing of certificates to vendors.
B) The completion of the baking task.
C) The festival they are organising.
D) The deadline for application.
questions.key_sentence: We're getting close to the deadline, however. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 时效提醒题。男士提醒申请截止日期（deadline）快到了。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: What does the man finally ask the woman to remember?
options: A) The closing date of submission.
B) The website of his company.
C) The details of the ceremony.
D) The organiser's address.
questions.key_sentence: Remember that the closing date is next Tuesday, the 28th of June. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 关键点强调题。男士最后特别叮嘱女士记住提交申请的截止日期（closing date）。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: Why has wild camping become popular in the UK?
options: A) Most scenic sites have been closed.
B) Access to official campsites is limited.
C) Health experts advise going outdoors.
D) People have more time during the summer.
questions.key_sentence: In part, this reflects the fact that official campsites have been wholly or partially closed, or are overflowing in a summer when fewer people are going abroad. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 原因归纳题。文中提到，官方露营地（official campsites）的部分关闭是露营流行的原因之一。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: Why is camping banned for part of the summer in public parks?
options: A) It is strongly opposed by nearby residents.
B) It leads to much waste of public money.
C) It has caused environmental concerns.
D) It has created conflicts among campers.
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。公园颁布禁令是因为露营者乱扔垃圾和排泄物，引发了环境担忧。 [cite: 1]
questions.key_sentence: Camping in public parks has now been banned for August and the early part of September because campers dump litter, human waste, and even their tents on the grassland. [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: What does the speaker suggest campers do?
options: A) Look for open land in Scotland.
B) Leave no trace of their camping.
C) Avoid getting close to wilderness.
D) Ask for permission from authorities.
questions.key_sentence: It would be common sense for people to use small tents and leave no trace of their visit. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 建议理解题。说话者建议露营者做到“不留痕迹”（leave no trace）。 [cite: 1]

questions.subcategory: 野生动物保护与生态系统
questions.audio_script: What does the passage say about pink dolphins?
options: A) They outcompete mythical creatures.
B) They usually mind their own business.
C) They truly exist in the Amazon region.
D) They resemble alarmingly large snakes.
questions.key_sentence: While this may seem like a mythical creature, pink dolphins do exist in the Amazon region. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 事实确认题。文章明确指出，粉红河豚确实存在于亚马逊地区。 [cite: 1]

questions.subcategory: 野生动物保护与生态系统
questions.audio_script: What is the unusual coloring of pink dolphins believed to originate in?
options: A) Scar tissue from dolphins' fighting.
B) Skin infection from water pollution.
C) Unhealed wounds from snake bites.
D) Swimming along in seasonal floods.
questions.key_sentence: Their unusual coloring is believed to be the result of scar tissue from dolphin fights whether playfighting or a serious bid for a mate. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 成因探究题。这种不寻常的颜色被认为是打斗留下的疤痕组织（scar tissue）。 [cite: 1]

questions.subcategory: 野生动物保护与生态系统
questions.audio_script: What has become of the Amazon wetland system since 2018?
options: A) It has been shrinking at an astonishing pace.
B) It has been placed under international protection.
C) It has been appealing to both freshwater and sea dolphins.
D) It has been abandoned as a battleground for male dolphins.
questions.key_sentence: The Amazon wetland system, fed by the Amazon River, is a crucial place for pink dolphins to breed, and since 2018 has been granted internationally protected status. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 事实细节题。自 2018 年起，亚马逊湿地系统已被授予国际保护地位（protected status）。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: What do we learn from a new survey by Merrill Lynch/Age Wave?
options: A) About 58% of young adults call parental support the new normal.
B) Most adult children enjoy increasing sources of financial support.
C) A full 70% of the young adults cannot afford to buy a car by themselves.
D) Most early adults cannot sustain their lifestyles without parental support.
questions.key_sentence: In a new Merrill Lynch/Age Wave survey, a full 70% of the early adults said they received financial support from their parents in the past year and 58% said they couldn't afford their current lifestyles without it. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 数据理解题。调查显示 58% 的受访者表示没有父母支持无法维持目前的生活方式。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: Why did most young adults in the survey say financial support to children aged 25 to 34 is a bad thing?
options: A) It renders them dependent.
B) It causes them to lose dignity.
C) It makes them mentally immature.
D) It hinders them from getting ahead.
questions.key_sentence: But 64% of the young adults surveyed said parents' financial support to children age 25 to 34 is "a bad thing", because it makes those kids dependent. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 观点理解题。受访者认为对 25-34 岁子女的资助会让其产生依赖性（dependent）。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: What did the respondents in the survey say regarding financial independence?
options: A) It challenges one's willpower.
B) It results from education.
C) It calls for due assistance.
D) It defines adulthood.
questions.key_sentence: In fact, the respondents said, financial independence defines adulthood. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 定义识别题。文中明确指出，受访者认为经济独立定义了成年（defines adulthood）。 [cite: 1]

questions.audio_script: What is one big reason young adults are struggling?
options: A) Current lifestyles.
B) Poor budgeting.
C) College loans.
D) Emergency expenses.
questions.key_sentence: One big reason they're struggling is attributed to college loans, of which the average amounts to $37,000. [cite: 1]
questions.explanation: 根源探究题。助学贷款（college loans）是年轻人奋斗挣扎的主要原因之一。 [cite: 1]

questions.subcategory: 生物技术与职业犬培育
